subject: Make babel run as root
commit_message:
Make babel run as root

Change-Id: I14a0e96ab0b1c4b4c04b8050ea853e74d31e94ce
Issue-ID: AAI-1179
Signed-off-by: Daniel Silverthorn <daniel.silverthorn@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-babel-master-verify-java/46/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-babel-master-verify-java/46

Patch Set 1:

Why has this change been made? Are there any security implications for allowing the container to run as root and/or allowing users to log into the image as root?

Is it an established ONAP convention for all images to run as root?

Patch Set 1:

> Why has this change been made? Are there any security implications
 > for allowing the container to run as root and/or allowing users to
 > log into the image as root?
 > 
 > Is it an established ONAP convention for all images to run as root?

It is the convention in AAI to have the containers run as root (babel is the only one that doesn't).  If it doesn't run as root, it means that the deployment infrastructure needs to ensure that the user that it does run as (aaiadmin) is created on the host VM.  I don't believe OOM does this ... which will mean that babel won't have permissions to write to the log volume.

I'm open to a discussion about having all containers run as something other than root (and then changing OOM to ensure that this user exists).  But for now, aligning babel with the rest of the services to run as root seems to make sense.

Patch Set 1:

> > Why has this change been made? Are there any security
 > implications
 > > for allowing the container to run as root and/or allowing users
 > to
 > > log into the image as root?
 > >
 > > Is it an established ONAP convention for all images to run as
 > root?
 > 
 > It is the convention in AAI to have the containers run as root
 > (babel is the only one that doesn't).  If it doesn't run as root,
 > it means that the deployment infrastructure needs to ensure that
 > the user that it does run as (aaiadmin) is created on the host VM. 
 > I don't believe OOM does this ... which will mean that babel won't
 > have permissions to write to the log volume.
 > 
 > I'm open to a discussion about having all containers run as
 > something other than root (and then changing OOM to ensure that
 > this user exists).  But for now, aligning babel with the rest of
 > the services to run as root seems to make sense.

I believe we just need to grant permission for the aaiadmin user to write to the log folder of the host VM. Allowing users to log into docker images as root has exposed security loopholes in the past, so we need to be careful.
Change has been successfully merged by Steven Blimkie

Patch Set 1:
